title: Derris angulata
intro: Derris angulata, also known as the Angelica plant, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Derris angulata include coumarins, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Derris angulata is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Derris angulata is classified in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family, which consists of a large group of flowering plants. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Derris. Derris angulata is a type of climbing vine native to tropical Asia. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Fabales, which is a group of plants characterized by having compound leaves. The classification of Derris angulata is also aligned with the APG III system, which is a widely accepted classification system for plants.
uses: Derris angulata is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's roots and leaves are employed in the preparation of herbal remedies. In some cultures, Derris angulata is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating pain and swelling. The plant's medicinal uses are often documented in folk medicine and traditional healing practices. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Derris angulata as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Derris angulata has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world, primarily for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions. Additionally, Derris angulata has been studied for its potential antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. properties: Derris angulata, also known as the angle-leaf Derris, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to possess antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential candidate for treating various health conditions. The plant's root and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. constituents: Derris angulata contains active constituents such as rotenone and other rotenoids, which are responsible for its insecticidal properties. These constituents are primarily found in the roots and stems of the plant. Rotenone is a potent insecticide that targets the nervous system of insects, ultimately leading to their death. The rotenoids present in Derris angulata are also known for their ability to repel pests and prevent infestations. The active constituents in Derris angulata have been used in traditional medicine and as a natural insecticide in various cultures.
parts: Derris angulata, the medicinal parts of the plant are primarily derived from its roots. Traditionally, the roots of Derris angulata have been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. preparations: Derris angulata is known for its traditional use in herbal medicine, particularly in South America.
